title: School Play in a Box
subtitle: A theatre arts after-school program
intro:
PYT is pleased to offer on-site, after-school drama classes for both public and private schools, called School Play in a Box (SPIB).

With our SPIB program, students learn basic acting skills, character development, team-building, storytelling, creativity, leadership skills, and more! It's also a great way for students to make new friends and build confidence.

Our instructors are experienced theatre professionals, and will meet with your students (up to 20 per class) once a week for an hour and a half at a time. During our meetings, we play theatre games, learn basic acting skills, and rehearse a script for a final performance.

The class culminates after 10 weeks with a play presented to family and friends.
pyt_provides: Script, instructor, props, sound cues, backdrops, simple costume suggestions, registration, flyer, and curriculum.
school_provides: A large space for rehearsals and performances, help publicizing the class and sharing info with parents and students, and a point person to liaise between the school and PYT. Parents should be available by email for updates and will be asked to provide a simple costume for their child (instructions provided).
inquiry_intro: For more information, or to inquire how to bring School Play in a Box to your school, email our Education Program Manager, Caitlyn DeRouin.
current_sessions_intro: For registration information, class dates, and other details regarding specific classes, please contact your school's front office, or click the link below. Please note that if your school has one of our programs and it is NOT listed on the registration page, it is because your school is solely handling registration through the school office.
